uk: 🕃 Зачаття чесного славного пророка, предтечі і хрестителя Господнього Івана
saints.en: Conception of the Honorable, Glorious Prophet, Forerunner and Baptist of the Lord, John

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» – 6 стихир Предтечі, Слава: Предтечі, І нині: богородичний-догмат за гласом «Слава». На стиховні – стихири Предтечі. Тропар Предтечі, Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря Предтечі. Відпуст середній з помин. служби дня і Предтечі.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар Предтечі (2), Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря Предтечі. Катизми чергові і сідальні Предтечі. Псалом 50. Канон Октоїха і Предтечі. По 3-ій пісні – сідальний Предтечі. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос Предтечі. По 9-ій – світильний Предтечі. Хвалитні стихири Предтечі. – 4. Слава, І нині: Предтечі. По великому славослов'ї з трисвятим – тропар Предтечі, Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря Предтечі. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби Предтечі.
Часи: на всіх – тропар і кондак Предтечі.
Літургія: антифони повсякденні. Тропар і кондак Предтечі. Ряд.: Ап. – Еф. 231 зач.; 5, 25-33. Єв. – Лк. 12 зач.; 4, 1-15. Предтечі: Ап. – Гал. 210 зач.; 4, 22-31. Єв. – Лк. 2 зач.; 1, 5-25.

troparion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate”)
Be glad, O barren one who has not given birth until now,  / for behold, you have conceived the lamp of the Sun,  / and He shall enlighten all the earth, which is afflicted with blindness.  / Dance, O Zachariah, and cry out with boldness: /  The Prophet of the Most High is about to be born.

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “The angelic choir...”)
The great and renowned Zachariah rejoices radiantly, /  and exults with his wife Elizabeth;  / for worthily does she conceive John the Baptist,  / whom the Archangel announced joyously, /  and men honor as an initiate of grace.

matched_oca_saint: Conception of the Honorable Glorious Prophet, Forerunner and Baptist John

oca_saints.name: Conception of the Honorable Glorious Prophet, Forerunner and Baptist John

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate”)
Be glad, O barren one who has not given birth until now,  / for behold, you have conceived the lamp of the Sun,  / and He shall enlighten all the earth, which is afflicted with blindness.  / Dance, O Zachariah, and cry out with boldness: /  The Prophet of the Most High is about to be born.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “The angelic choir...”)
The great and renowned Zachariah rejoices radiantly, /  and exults with his wife Elizabeth;  / for worthily does she conceive John the Baptist,  / whom the Archangel announced joyously, /  and men honor as an initiate of grace.

oca_saints.name: Saints Xanthippi and Polyxeni of Spain

troparion.text: You received the light of the divine Faith,  /  and your works shone with piety, O divinely-inspired Polyxénē; /   you glorified Christ in your life /   as well as by your blameless actions; /   therefore intercede with the Lord Who glorified you, to grant us great mercy.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
Passing through the countries of the earth most wondrously, /  you were shown holy women like yourselves, O godly Xanthίppē and Polyxénē; /  therefore you received unfading crowns from the hands of our Savior, /  now hear those who cry: “Rejoice, most godly pair.”

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Joseph was amazed...”)
Your lamb Irais, / calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / “I love You, my Bridegroom, / and in seeking You, I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love.” / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Finding your most revered temple to be a source of healing for our souls,  / we the faithful cry out to You with a loud voice:  / “O most renowned Virgin Martyr Irais,  / entreat Christ God unceasingly on behalf of us all.”

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Andrew, John, and John’s children: Peter and Antonius, of Syracuse, martyred in Africa

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!
